# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0031 - QUIC CRT client-side ServerHello branch-point architecture

## Purpose

Describe the smallest honest ServerHello branch-point seam: the managed client/runtime path classifies a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt as accepted or rejected, sends rejected attempts back through the existing full-handshake path without corrupting state, and does not claim resumed-handshake success yet.

## Scope

This slice covers only client-side ServerHello branch-point classification for a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt. Rejected attempts must rejoin the existing full-handshake path without corrupting state. Accepted attempts are recognized but do not claim resumed-handshake success, abbreviated-handshake completion, or any 0-RTT behavior.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0133

## Design Summary

The managed client/runtime path already owns opaque resumption material and can build a PSK-capable ClientHello attempt. This slice extends the server-side acceptance seam just enough to read the ServerHello pre_shared_key selection, classify the attempt as accepted or rejected, and keep the rejected branch on the existing full-handshake path. Accepted attempts are recognized but do not claim abbreviated-handshake completion, and early data remains closed.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicDetachedResumptionTicketSnapshot.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicPostHandshakeTicketTestSupport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0132.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0133.cs

## Risks

- If the ServerHello pre_shared_key signal is not parsed from the transcript, accepted attempts cannot be distinguished from rejected ones.
- If rejection does not fall back through the existing full-handshake path, the slice would silently corrupt state or poison the bootstrap path.
- If accepted attempts are treated as resumed-handshake success, the runtime would overclaim behavior that the repository has not yet proved.
- If the branch outcome leaks into public API or IsSupported, the slice would widen beyond the narrow proof floor.
